dependencies: Reliance on Amazon Web Services (AWS) for cloud computing infrastructure
Dependence on content delivery networks (CDN) including proprietary Open Connect network
Reliance on Internet Service Providers (ISPs) for content delivery
Dependence on consumer electronics manufacturers, MVPDs, mobile operators for device access
Reliance on third-party payment processors for member billing
risks: AWS disruption could impact operations significantly
Cannot easily switch AWS operations to another cloud provider
ISPs may block, restrict or charge for network access
Network operators may implement discriminatory practices
Payment processing disruptions could adversely impact revenue
Reliance on third-party technology and services for operations

regulatory_risks: Growing regulatory action on media landscape and internet content delivery
Cultural support legislation updates including content quotas, levies and investment obligations
Ownership restrictions on service and content in certain countries
Formal content reviews and adjustments required by regulators in certain jurisdictions
EU Audio Visual Media Services Directive requiring 30% European works in catalogs
EU Member States imposing additional investment obligations and levies
Data protection laws including GDPR and California Privacy Rights Act (CPRA)
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and UK Bribery Act compliance
Export controls and economic sanctions compliance
Intellectual property laws and anti-piracy enforcement challenges
Tax law changes and interpretations in multiple jurisdictions
Data localization and data transfer restrictions
Consumer protection laws for online commerce
Payment card industry data security standards
Collective bargaining agreements in motion picture industry

sanction_risks: Potential retaliatory action from governments on controversial content
